description: Curated LinkedIn / recent activity. Synced to Supabase as FAQs (and projects where noted). See docs/knowledge-organization.md for types.

entries.title: FlowHouse Cohort 1 (Prettiflow)
entries.summary: Selected for FlowHouse Cohort 1, a private builder cohort by Prettiflow with credits and resources from partners including NVIDIA, OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, Azure, and PostHog.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Recent programs, what Nikhil is doing now, builder communities.
agent_guidance.clarify: This is a cohort selection, not a product Nikhil built.

entries.title: Medicaid Analytics Agent (Amazon Nova AI Hackathon 2026)
entries.summary: Built an agent to chat with US Medicaid provider-level claims data (2018–2024) in plain English—tables and charts for questions about providers, billing codes, and trends. Stack: AWS Bedrock Nova 2 Lite, Snowflake. Built for Amazon Nova AI Hackathon 2026.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Hackathons, AI agents, healthcare data, AWS/Nova projects, recent builds.
agent_guidance.clarify: This is a hackathon project using public Medicaid spending data—not clinical care software.

project_payload.name: Medicaid Analytics Agent
project_payload.one_liner: Chat with US Medicaid provider claims data in plain English—built for Amazon Nova AI Hackathon 2026.
project_payload.problem: New large public Medicaid datasets are hard for non-analysts to explore quickly.
project_payload.solution: An AI agent that answers natural-language questions with tables and charts over Snowflake-hosted claims data, using AWS Bedrock Nova for reasoning.
project_payload.why_it_matters: Shows Nikhil can ship agentic analytics on real public datasets under hackathon constraints.

entries.title: Amazon Nova AI Hackathon — Winner board
entries.summary: Recognized on the winner board for the Amazon Nova AI Hackathon (Devpost/AWS). Not the top prize tier he hoped for, but the submission (Medicaid Analytics Agent) was acknowledged.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Hackathon wins, recent achievements, Amazon Nova.
agent_guidance.clarify: Winner board recognition—not the highest prize. Pair with the Medicaid Analytics Agent project for context.

entries.title: Amazon Nova AI Hackathon — AWS credits
entries.summary: Received AWS credits from the Amazon Nova AI Hackathon organizers (follow-up to the same hackathon as the Medicaid Analytics Agent submission).

agent_guidance.clarify: Same Amazon Nova hackathon arc as the Medicaid agent—credits award, not a new project.

entries.title: Ramudu-Sita (online Telugu party game)
entries.summary: Rebuilt a childhood Telugu party game (Ramudu-Sita: chits, roles, guessing, bluffing) as a browser game in ~4 hours so cousins in different cities can play together—with video/audio calls and chat via Anything.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Side projects, games, Telugu/cultural projects, fast builds, family/social products.
agent_guidance.clarify: A nostalgic social game—not a hackathon or job project.

project_payload.name: Ramudu-Sita
project_payload.one_liner: Online version of a Telugu childhood party game with video, audio, chat, and secret roles.
project_payload.problem: Family spread across cities couldn't easily replay a favorite in-person guessing game.
project_payload.solution: Browser-based Ramudu-Sita with secret roles, bluffing, and built-in video/audio calls.
project_payload.why_it_matters: Shows fast 0→1 product sense and building for real human/family use cases, not only résumé projects.

entries.title: Google learning badge — AI agents with ADK
entries.summary: Completed Google learning on building AI agents with ADK: agentic patterns, multi-agent workflows, memory, and MCP—practical tool-connected agents, not theory only.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Learning, Google Cloud, agents, MCP, Talk with Nikhil stack (ADK/Gemini).
agent_guidance.clarify: A learning badge/course completion—relevant to how Talk with Nikhil is built.

entries.title: Shared post about Anthropic Claude certification (not Nikhil's cert)
entries.summary: LinkedIn post sharing that Anthropic opened Claude Certified Architect / Academy paths (API, Claude Code, MCP, Vertex, Bedrock)—community/news, not a credential Nikhil earned.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Rarely—only if asked about Claude certifications in general.
agent_guidance.clarify: He shared news about the program; he did NOT claim to have the certification himself.

entries.title: ClawdTalk & Rime workshop (San Francisco)
entries.summary: Attended and demoed at a ClawdTalk & Rime workshop in SF—OpenClaw agents, Clawdtalk voice wiring, Rime voice range, Telnyx use cases.

agent_guidance.when_to_mention: Voice AI events, OpenClaw, SF tech community, Telnyx.
agent_guidance.clarify: Attendance/demo at a workshop—not a shipped product launch.

entries.title: LinkedIn clone with Bolt.new (overnight experiment)
entries.summary: Used bolt.new credits to build a LinkedIn-style clone in ~6 hours (9 PM start)—imperfect but real; learned to plan earlier after deployment friction.

agent_guidance.clarify: A credit-burn learning sprint—not core portfolio like Talk with Nikhil or Medicaid agent.

project_payload.name: LinkedIn Clone (Bolt.new)
project_payload.one_liner: Overnight LinkedIn UI clone built with bolt.new to experiment with speed vs polish.
project_payload.problem: Wanted to stress-test rapid no-code/full-stack generation limits.
project_payload.solution: Built a working clone in one evening; documented deployment lessons.
project_payload.why_it_matters: Shows willingness to experiment quickly; lower priority than agent/hackathon work.
